name: Abu Bakr ibn Ali
title: Hashemite Warrior
shortDescription: One of the brave sons of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and a brother of Hazrat Abbas (AS) who sacrificed his life defending the Master of Martyrs.
fullStory:
Abu Bakr (Abdullah) ibn Ali (AS) is one of the brave sons of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and Layla bint Mas'ud. He was a brave and devoted brother of Imam Hussain (AS) who stood by his brother's side during the event of Karbala.

On the day of Ashura, after the martyrdom of many Ansar companions, it was the turn of the Hashemite youth. Abu Bakr ibn Ali was among the first to seek permission from Imam Hussain (AS) to enter the battlefield. Despite his youth, he possessed Haidari bravery and, reciting epic war poetry, charged into the enemy ranks.

On the battlefield, he fought with absolute valor, killing a number of Kufan warriors. Umar Sa'd's soldiers, overwhelmed by his bravery, surrounded him collectively.

Finally, while his body was a target for swords and spears, Abu Bakr ibn Ali was martyred by Zajr ibn Qays or Uqba ibn Ghanawi, and his pure soul flew to the heavens.
quoteArabic: شَيْخي عَلِيٌّ ذُو الفِعالِ الأَطْهَرِ، مِنْ هاشِمٍ فِي الذِّرْوَةِ المُشْتَهِرِ...
quote: (In his war poetry:) My father is Ali, the master of pure deeds, from Hashem, who are at the pinnacle of fame...
